similar_questions.title: Maximize Y‑Sum by Picking a Triplet of Distinct X‑Values

discussion_posts.name: Oterman
discussion_posts.content: what a fucccccccccccccck!\n\nmake me verrrrrry angry!

discussion_posts.name: ngtrhieu
discussion_posts.content:
If you are struggle to understand the problem, read this:

---

You are given a sets of `N` items, each item have a value `values[i]` and a label `labels[i]`.

You will need to pick out some items in the set. Being greedy, you want to **maximize the total values of your items** while following these constraints:

- You can only choose maximum `M` items.
- You can only choose maximum `L` items of the same label.

Return the maximum value of the items you pick.

**Note:**
1. `1 <= N <= 20,000`
2. `0 <= values[i], labels[i] <= 20,000`
3. `1 <= M, L <= N`

---
`N` is `values.length` or `labels.length`
`M` is the `num_wanted`
`L` is the `use_limit`

discussion_posts.name: singhpratapvarang
discussion_posts.content: \uD83E\uDDD9 Yoda\'s Wisdom: \u201CFrom many labels, strong values rise. But overuse, do not. Strongest total, balance brings.\u201D\n\nhttps://leetcode.com/problems/largest-values-from-labels/solutions/6706492/largest-values-from-labels-choose-wisely-ksgx

discussion_posts.name: HansarajKosarkar
discussion_posts.content: Greedily choose maximum values from different labels without exceeding useLimit. Need to use suitable data structure `vector<pair<int,int>>val_labels;` to store `{values[i], labels[i]}`

discussion_posts.name: to_ro_to_ki
discussion_posts.content: Most of people here discuss the greedy approach, but what happens if `values = [10, 10, 9], labels = [0, 1, 1], wanted = 2, limit = 1`?\nI suppose the third element would not be picked up.

discussion_posts.name: panzerschrek
discussion_posts.content: WTF? How a subset problem (with exponential natural time complexity) can be labeled as "medium"?
